31	@dataclass
32	class GuidedDecodingParams:
33	    """One of these fields will be used to build a logit processor."""
34	    json: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None
35	    regex: Optional[str] = None
36	    choice: Optional[list[str]] = None
37	    grammar: Optional[str] = None
38	    json_object: Optional[bool] = None
39	    """These are other options that can be set"""
40	    backend: Optional[str] = None
41	    backend_was_auto: bool = False
42	    disable_fallback: bool = False
43	    disable_any_whitespace: bool = False
44	    disable_additional_properties: bool = False
45	    whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
46	    structural_tag: Optional[str] = None
47	
48	    @staticmethod
49	    def from_optional(
50	        json: Optional[Union[dict, BaseModel, str]] = None,
51	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
52	        choice: Optional[list[str]] = None,
53	        grammar: Optional[str] = None,
54	        json_object: Optional[bool] = None,
55	        backend: Optional[str] = None,
56	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
57	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
58	    ) -> Optional["GuidedDecodingParams"]:
59	        if all(arg is None for arg in (json, regex, choice, grammar,
60	                                       json_object, structural_tag)):
61	            return None
62	        # Extract json schemas from pydantic models
63	        if isinstance(json, (BaseModel, type(BaseModel))):
64	            json = json.model_json_schema()
65	        return GuidedDecodingParams(
66	            json=json,
67	            regex=regex,
68	            choice=choice,
69	            grammar=grammar,
70	            json_object=json_object,
71	            backend=backend,
72	            whitespace_pattern=whitespace_pattern,
73	            structural_tag=structural_tag,
74	        )
75	
76	    def __post_init__(self):
77	        """Validate that some fields are mutually exclusive."""
78	        guide_count = sum([
79	            self.json is not None, self.regex is not None, self.choice
80	            is not None, self.grammar is not None, self.json_object is not None
81	        ])
82	        if guide_count > 1:
83	            raise ValueError(
84	                "You can only use one kind of guided decoding but multiple are "
85	                f"specified: {self.__dict__}")
86	
87	        if self.backend is not None and ":" in self.backend:
88	            self._extract_backend_options()
89	
90	    @deprecated(
91	        "Passing guided decoding backend options inside backend in the format "
92	        "'backend:...' is deprecated. This will be removed in v0.10.0. Please "
93	        "use the dedicated arguments '--disable-fallback', "
94	        "'--disable-any-whitespace' and '--disable-additional-properties' "
95	        "instead.")
96	    def _extract_backend_options(self):
97	        """Extract backend options from the backend string."""
98	        assert isinstance(self.backend, str)
99	        self.backend, options = self.backend.split(":")
100	        options_set = set(options.strip().split(","))
101	        if "no-fallback" in options_set:
102	            self.disable_fallback = True
103	        if "disable-any-whitespace" in options_set:
104	            self.disable_any_whitespace = True
105	        if "no-additional-properties" in options_set:
106	            self.disable_additional_properties = True

109	class RequestOutputKind(Enum):
110	    # Return entire output so far in every RequestOutput
111	    CUMULATIVE = 0
112	    # Return only deltas in each RequestOutput
113	    DELTA = 1
114	    # Do not return intermediate RequestOutput
115	    FINAL_ONLY = 2

117	
118	class SamplingParams(
119	        msgspec.Struct,
120	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
121	        # required for @cached_property.
122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
158	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
159	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
160	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
161	            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
162	            the stop tokens are special tokens.
163	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
164	            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
165	            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
166	            can complete the sequence.
167	        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
168	            output text. Defaults to False.
169	        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
170	            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
171	        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
172	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
173	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
174	        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
175	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
176	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
177	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
178	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
179	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
180	            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
181	        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
182	        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
183	        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
184	        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
185	            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
186	        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
187	            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
188	            a first argument.
189	        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
190	            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
191	            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
192	            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
193	        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
194	            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
195	        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
196	            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
197	        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
198	            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
199	            Defaults to None.
200	        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
201	            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
202	            sampling implementations.
203	    """
204	
205	    n: int = 1
206	    best_of: Optional[int] = None
207	    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
208	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
209	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
210	    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
211	    temperature: float = 1.0
212	    top_p: float = 1.0
213	    top_k: int = 0
214	    min_p: float = 0.0
215	    seed: Optional[int] = None
216	    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
217	    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
218	    ignore_eos: bool = False
219	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
220	    min_tokens: int = 0
221	    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
222	    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
223	    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
224	    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
225	    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
226	    detokenize: bool = True
227	    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
228	    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
229	    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
230	    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
231	    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
232	    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
233	    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
234	    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE
235	
236	    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
237	    # They are set in post_init.
238	    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
239	    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)
240	
241	    # Fields used to construct logits processors
242	    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
243	    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
244	    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
245	    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
246	
247	    # Fields used for bad words
248	    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
249	    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None
250	
251	    @staticmethod
252	    def from_optional(
253	        n: Optional[int] = 1,
254	        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
255	        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
256	        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
257	        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
258	        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
259	        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
260	        top_k: int = 0,
261	        min_p: float = 0.0,
262	        seed: Optional[int] = None,
263	        stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None,
264	        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
265	        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
266	        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
267	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
268	        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,
269	        min_tokens: int = 0,
270	        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
271	        prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
272	        detokenize: bool = True,
273	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
274	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
275	        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,
276	        truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int,
277	                                                   msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None,
278	        output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE,
279	        guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None,
280	        logit_bias: Optional[Union[dict[int, float], dict[str, float]]] = None,
281	        allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
282	        extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
283	    ) -> "SamplingParams":
284	        if logit_bias is not None:
285	            # Convert token_id to integer
286	            # Clamp the bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI API spec
287	            logit_bias = {
288	                int(token): min(100.0, max(-100.0, bias))

292	        return SamplingParams(

295	            presence_penalty=0.0

297	            frequency_penalty=0.0
298	            if frequency_penalty is None else frequency_penalty,
299	            repetition_penalty=1.0
300	            if repetition_penalty is None else repetition_penalty,
301	            temperature=1.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
302	            top_p=1.0 if top_p is None else top_p,
303	            top_k=top_k,
304	            min_p=min_p,
305	            seed=seed,
306	            stop=stop,
307	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
308	            bad_words=bad_words,
309	            include_stop_str_in_output=include_stop_str_in_output,
310	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
311	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
312	            min_tokens=min_tokens,
313	            logprobs=logprobs,

315	            detokenize=detokenize,

318	            logits_processors=logits_processors,

327	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
328	        # how we deal with `best_of``:
329	        # if `best_of`` is not set, we default to `n`;
330	        # if `best_of`` is set, we set `n`` to `best_of`,
331	        # and set `_real_n`` to the original `n`.
332	        # when we return the result, we will check
333	        # if we need to return `n` or `_real_n` results
334	        if self.best_of:
335	            if self.best_of < self.n:
336	                raise ValueError(
337	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
338	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
339	            if not self._real_n:
340	                self._real_n = self.n
341	                self.n = self.best_of
342	
343	        if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:
344	            logger.warning(
345	                "temperature %s is less than %s, which may cause numerical "
346	                "errors nan or inf in tensors. We have maxed it out to %s.",
347	                self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP, _MAX_TEMP)
348	            self.temperature = max(self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP)
349	
350	        if self.seed == -1:
351	            self.seed = None
352	
353	        if self.stop is None:
354	            self.stop = []
355	        elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
356	            self.stop = [self.stop]
357	
358	        if self.stop_token_ids is None:
359	            self.stop_token_ids = []
360	
361	        if self.bad_words is None:
362	            self.bad_words = []
363	
364	        if self.logprobs is True:
365	            self.logprobs = 1
366	
367	        if self.prompt_logprobs is True:
368	            self.prompt_logprobs = 1

370	        # Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation
371	        # until sequence is finished.
372	        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:
373	            self.output_text_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in self.stop) - 1

375	        self._verify_args()
376	
377	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
378	            # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
379	            self.top_p = 1.0
380	            self.top_k = 0
381	            self.min_p = 0.0
382	            self._verify_greedy_sampling()

384	        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
385	        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)

387	    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
388	        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
389	            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
390	                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
391	        if self.n < 1:
392	            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
393	        if self.best_of is not None:
394	            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
395	                raise ValueError(
396	                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
397	            if self.best_of < 1:
398	                raise ValueError(
399	                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
400	            if self.best_of < self.n:
401	                raise ValueError(
402	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
403	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
404	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
405	            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
406	                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
407	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
408	            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
409	                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
410	        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
411	            raise ValueError(
412	                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
413	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
414	        if self.temperature < 0.0:

416	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
417	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:

495	    def update_from_tokenizer(self, tokenizer: AnyTokenizer) -> None:

508	
509	                # If no space at the beginning
510	                # or if prefix space produces a new word token

518	        invalid_token_ids = [
519	            token_id for bad_words_token_ids in self._bad_words_token_ids
520	            for token_id in bad_words_token_ids
521	            if token_id < 0 or token_id > tokenizer.max_token_id

526	                f" but the following tokens"
527	                f" were specified as bad: {invalid_token_ids}."
528	                f" All token id values should be integers satisfying:"
529	                f" 0 <= token_id <= {tokenizer.max_token_id}.")

548	    def clone(self) -> "SamplingParams":
549	        """Deep copy, but maybe not the LogitsProcessor objects.

552	        data that is expensive to copy. However, if not copied, the processor

559	            for lp in self.logits_processors

85	class SamplerOutput(
86	        msgspec.Struct,
87	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
88	        array_like=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
89	    """For each sequence group, we generate a list of SequenceOutput object,
90	    each of which contains one possible candidate for the next token.
91	
92	    This data structure implements methods, so it can be used like a list, but
93	    also has optional fields for device tensors.
94	    """
95	
96	    outputs: list[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]
97	
98	    # On-device tensor containing probabilities of each token.
99	    sampled_token_probs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
100	
101	    # On-device tensor containing the logprobs of each token.
102	    logprobs: Optional["torch.Tensor"] = None
103	
104	    # Holds either (1) the pythonized sampler result (single-step scheduling)
105	    # or (2) what will be arguments for later deferred pythonization of the
106	    # sampler result (muliti-step scheduling)
107	    deferred_sample_results_args: Optional[SampleResultArgsType] = None
108	
109	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token ids.
110	    sampled_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
111	    # CPU tensor containing the sampled token ids. Used during multi-step to
112	    # return the sampled token ids from last rank to AsyncLLMEngine to be
113	    # 'broadcasted' to all other PP ranks for next step.
114	    sampled_token_ids_cpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
115	
116	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token embeddings (embeddings
117	    # corresponding to the sampled token ids). Used when prompt embeddings are
118	    # specified in lieu of prompt token ids or text.
119	    sampled_token_embeds: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
120	
121	    # Spec decode metrics populated by workers.
122	    spec_decode_worker_metrics: Optional[SpecDecodeWorkerMetrics] = None
123	
124	    # Optional last hidden states from the model.
125	    hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
126	
127	    # Optional prefill hidden states from the model
128	    # (used for models like EAGLE).
129	    prefill_hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
130	
131	    # Time taken in the forward pass for this across all workers
132	    model_forward_time: Optional[float] = None
133	
134	    # Time taken in the model execute function. This will include model forward,
135	    # block/sync across workers, cpu-gpu sync time and sampling time.
136	    model_execute_time: Optional[float] = None

138	    def __getitem__(self, idx: int) -> CompletionSequenceGroupOutput:
139	        return self.outputs[idx]

141	    def __setitem__(self, idx: int, value):

157	        sampled_token_probs_repr = ("None" if self.sampled_token_probs is None
158	                                    else self.sampled_token_probs.shape)

160	                                  self.sampled_token_ids.shape)
161	        return (
162	            f"SamplerOutput(outputs={self.outputs}, "
163	            f"sampled_token_probs={sampled_token_probs_repr}, "
164	            f"sampled_token_ids={sampled_token_ids_repr}, "

168	class Sampler(nn.Module):
169	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
170	
171	    This layer does the following:
172	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
173	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
174	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
175	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
176	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
177	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
178	    6. Sample the next tokens.
179	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
180	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
181	
182	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
183	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
184	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
185	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
186	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
187	    """
188	
189	    def __init__(self):
190	        super().__init__()
191	
192	        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
193	        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
194	        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
195	        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
196	        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False
197	
198	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
199	        self,
200	        logits: torch.Tensor,
201	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
202	    ):
203	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
204	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
205	        decodes of the same sequences.
206	        """
207	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
208	
209	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
210	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
211	        # have pinned memory.
212	        self._sampling_tensors = None
213	
214	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
215	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
216	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
217	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
218	
219	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
220	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
221	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
222	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
223	
224	    def forward(
225	        self,
226	        logits: torch.Tensor,
227	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
228	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
229	        """
230	        Single-step scheduling:
231	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
232	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
233	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
234	
235	        Multi-step scheduling:
236	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
237	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
238	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
239	            tensor
240	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
241	            in the
242	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
243	            structure
244	
245	        Args:
246	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
247	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
248	        """
249	        assert logits is not None
250	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
251	
252	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
253	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
254	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
255	        elif self._do_penalties:
256	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
257	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
258	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
259	            # between decode runs.
260	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
261	
262	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
263	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
264	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
265	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
266	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
267	
268	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
269	
270	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
271	        if do_penalties:
272	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
273	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
277	
278	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
279	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
280	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
281	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
282	
283	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
284	            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
285	                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)
286	
287	        if do_min_p:
288	            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
289	
290	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
291	        # Compute the probabilities.
292	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
293	        # Compute the log probabilities.
294	        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
295	
296	        # Sample the next tokens.
297	        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
298	            probs,
299	            logprobs,

33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).

36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:

51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)

58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).

118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)

133	            greedy_sampled,

351	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
352	    logits: torch.Tensor,
353	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
354	) -> torch.Tensor:
355	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
356	        have not been generated yet
357	    """
358	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
359	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
360	    logits_applied = 0
361	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
362	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
363	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
364	
365	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
366	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
367	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
368	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
369	            continue
370	
371	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
372	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
373	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
374	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
375	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
376	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
377	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
378	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
379	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
380	
381	            if seqs_to_penalize:
382	                # convert to the index into logits
383	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
384	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
385	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
386	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
387	
388	    if logits_to_penalize:
389	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
390	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
391	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
392	
393	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
394	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
395	    return logits

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits

427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits

444	def _greedy_sample(
445	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
446	    samples: torch.Tensor,
447	) -> SampleResultType:
448	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
449	
450	    Args:
451	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
453	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
454	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
455	    Returns:
456	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
457	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
458	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
459	    """
460	    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
461	    sample_idx = 0
462	    results: SampleResultType = []
463	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
464	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
465	            results.append(([], []))
466	            continue
467	
468	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
469	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
470	        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
471	            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
472	        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
473	        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
474	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
475	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
476	    return results

479	def _random_sample(
480	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
481	    random_samples: torch.Tensor,
482	) -> SampleResultType:
483	    """Run random sampling on a given samples.
484	
485	    Args:
486	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
487	        random_samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The
488	            length of samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
489	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
490	    Returns:
491	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
492	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
493	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
494	    """
495	    # Find the maximum n value of the prompt phase requests.
496	    random_samples = random_samples.cpu()
497	    sample_idx = 0
498	    results: SampleResultType = []
499	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
500	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
501	            results.append(([], []))
502	            continue
503	
504	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
505	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
506	        is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
507	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
508	        if is_prompt:
509	            # Prompt phase.
510	            parent_ids = [0] * sampling_params.n
511	            next_token_ids = random_samples[
512	                sample_idx, :sampling_params.n].tolist()
513	        else:
514	            # Generation phase.
515	            parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
516	            next_token_ids = random_samples[sample_idx:sample_idx +
517	                                            num_parent_seqs, 0].tolist()
518	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
519	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
520	    return results

523	# torch.multinomial forces a GPU<->CPU sync.
524	# Therefore, we use an optimized implementation instead.
525	# Note that we always sample with replacement.
526	# probs will be modified in place, but this is fine, as we pass
527	# in a copy already.
528	def _multinomial(
529	    probs: torch.Tensor,
530	    num_samples: int,
531	    seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]] = None,
532	) -> torch.Tensor:
533	    if num_samples > 1:
534	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
535	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
536	    if seq_groups is None:
537	        q.exponential_()
538	    else:
539	        sample_idx = 0
540	        for seq_group in seq_groups:
541	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
542	            stride = len(seq_ids) * num_samples
543	            assert seq_group.generator is not None
544	            q[sample_idx:sample_idx +
545	              stride].exponential_(generator=seq_group.generator)
546	            sample_idx += stride
547	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=1).view(-1, num_samples)
548

550	def _top_k_top_p_multinomial_with_flashinfer(
551	        probs: torch.Tensor, top_ks: torch.Tensor, top_ps: torch.Tensor,
552	        num_samples: int, seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]]):
553	    if num_samples > 1:
554	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
555	        top_ks = top_ks.repeat_interleave(num_samples)
556	        top_ps = top_ps.repeat_interleave(num_samples)
557	    batch_next_token_ids = flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling(
558	        probs,
559	        top_ks,
560	        top_ps,
561	    )
562	    return batch_next_token_ids.view(-1, num_samples)

565	def get_pythonized_sample_results(
566	        sample_result_args: SampleResultArgsType) -> SampleResultType:
567	    '''This function consumes GPU-side sampler results and computes
568	    Pythonized CPU-side sampler results (GPU -> CPU sync.)
569	
570	    Single-step scheduling: this function is invoked at sampling-time
571	    for immediate Pythonization.
572	
573	    Multi-step scheduling: Pythonization is deferred until after multiple
574	    GPU-side steps have been completed.
575	
576	    Args:
577	      sample_result_args: GPU-side inputs to the Pythonization process
578	
579	    Returns:
580	      Pythonized sampler results
581	    '''
582	
583	    (
584	        sample_metadata,
585	        sampling_metadata,
586	        greedy_samples,
587	        multinomial_samples,
588	        sample_results_dict,
589	    ) = (
590	        sample_result_args.sample_metadata,
591	        sample_result_args.sampling_metadata,
592	        sample_result_args.greedy_samples,
593	        sample_result_args.multinomial_samples,
594	        sample_result_args.sample_results_dict,
595	    )
596	
597	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
598	        if sampling_type not in sample_metadata:
599	            continue
600	        (seq_group_id, seq_groups) = sample_metadata[sampling_type]
601	        if sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
602	            sample_results = _greedy_sample(seq_groups, greedy_samples)
603	        elif sampling_type in (SamplingType.RANDOM, SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED):
604	            sample_results = _random_sample(seq_groups,
605	                                            multinomial_samples[sampling_type])
606	        sample_results_dict.update(zip(seq_group_id, sample_results))
607	
608	    return [
609	        sample_results_dict.get(i, ([], []))
610	        for i in range(len(sampling_metadata.seq_groups))
611	    ]

652	                                              VLLM_INVALID_TOKEN_ID,
653	                                              dtype=torch.long,
654	                                              device=logprobs.device)
655	    else:

658	    # Counterintiutively, having two loops here is actually faster.
659	    # The first loop can run without waiting on GPU<->CPU sync.

33	class OpenAIBaseModel(BaseModel):
34	    # OpenAI API does allow extra fields
35	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
36	
37	    # Cache class field names
38	    field_names: ClassVar[Optional[set[str]]] = None
39	
40	    @model_validator(mode="wrap")
41	    @classmethod
42	    def __log_extra_fields__(cls, data, handler):
43	        result = handler(data)
44	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
45	            return result
46	        field_names = cls.field_names
47	        if field_names is None:
48	            # Get all class field names and their potential aliases
49	            field_names = set()
50	            for field_name, field in cls.model_fields.items():
51	                field_names.add(field_name)
52	                if alias := getattr(field, "alias", None):
53	                    field_names.add(alias)
54	            cls.field_names = field_names
55	
56	        # Compare against both field names and aliases
57	        if any(k not in field_names for k in data):
58	            logger.warning(
59	                "The following fields were present in the request "
60	                "but ignored: %s",
61	                data.keys() - field_names,
62	            )
63	        return result

768	def _get_ranks(x: torch.Tensor, indices: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
769	    """
770	    This function calculates the ranks of the chosen tokens in a logprob tensor.
771	
772	    Args:
773	        x (torch.Tensor): 2D logprob tensor of shape (N, M)
774	                        where N is the no. of tokens and M is the vocab dim.
775	        indices (torch.Tensor): List of chosen token indices.
776	
777	    Returns:
778	        torch.Tensor: 1D tensor of shape (N,) where N is the no. of tokens.
779	                    Each element in the returned tensor represents the rank
780	                    of the chosen token in the input logprob tensor.
781	    """
782	    vals = x[torch.arange(0, len(x), device=x.device, dtype=indices.dtype),
783	             indices]
784	    result = (x > vals[:, None])
785	    del vals
786	    return result.sum(1).add_(1)

917	        prompt_logprobs_per_seq_group.append(prompt_logprobs)
918	
919	        (sampled_logprobs, top_logprob_idx,
920	         selected_logprobs_idx) = _get_sampled_logprob_if_needed(
921	             seq_group, sample_result, selected_logprobs, ranks, top_token_ids,
922	             top_logprobs, selected_logprobs_idx, top_logprob_idx)
923	        sample_logprobs_per_seq_group.append(sampled_logprobs)
924	
925	    return prompt_logprobs_per_seq_group, sample_logprobs_per_seq_group

927	
928	def _get_prompt_logprob_if_needed(
929	    seq_group: SequenceGroupToSample,
930	    selected_logprobs: torch.Tensor,
931	    ranks: torch.Tensor,
932	    top_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
933	    top_logprobs: torch.Tensor,
934	    selected_logprobs_idx: int,
935	    top_logprob_idx: int,
936	):
937	    """Compute the prompt logprob from a sequence group if needed."""
938	    sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
939	    is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
940	
941	    # Find prompt logprobs
942	    prompt_logprobs: Optional[PromptLogprobs] = None
943	    if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
944	        prompt_logprobs = []
945	        num_logprobs = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs
946	        next_prompt_tokens = _get_next_prompt_tokens(seq_group)
947	        # Pre-select indexes and create a list. It is faster than calling .item
948	        # repetitively.
949	        selected_logprob_items = selected_logprobs[
950	            selected_logprobs_idx:selected_logprobs_idx +
951	            len(next_prompt_tokens)].tolist()
952	        rank_items = ranks[selected_logprobs_idx:selected_logprobs_idx +
953	                           len(next_prompt_tokens)].tolist()
954	
955	        for idx, token_id in enumerate(next_prompt_tokens):
956	            # Calculate the prompt logprob of the real prompt tokens.
957	            # {token_id: (logprob, rank_from_vocab)}
958	            prompt_logprobs_dict: dict[int, tuple[float, int]] = {
959	                token_id: (selected_logprob_items[idx], rank_items[idx])
960	            }
961	
962	            # Add top K prompt logprobs along with its rank.
963	            if num_logprobs > 0:
964	                top_ids = top_token_ids[
965	                    top_logprob_idx, :num_logprobs].tolist()
966	                top_probs = top_logprobs[
967	                    top_logprob_idx, :num_logprobs].tolist()
968	                # Top K is already sorted by rank, so we can use 1 ~
969	                # num_logprobs + 1 for rank.
970	                top_ranks = range(1, num_logprobs + 1)
971	                prompt_logprobs_dict.update({
972	                    top_id: (top_prob, rank)
973	                    for top_id, top_prob, rank in zip(top_ids, top_probs,
974	                                                      top_ranks)
975	                })
976	            prompt_logprobs.append({
977	                token_id: Logprob(*logprob_and_rank)
978	                for token_id, logprob_and_rank in prompt_logprobs_dict.items()
979	            })
980	            # + 1 to go to the next prompt token.
981	            top_logprob_idx += 1
982	
983	        # + len(next_prompt_tokens) to go to the next prompt.
984	        selected_logprobs_idx += len(next_prompt_tokens)
985	    return prompt_logprobs, top_logprob_idx, selected_logprobs_idx
986

999	    seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids

20	class MoveDirectionality(Enum):
21	    # One-way i1->i2 req move within batch
22	    UNIDIRECTIONAL = 0
23	    # Two-way i1<->i2 req swap within batch
24	    SWAP = 1
25	
26	
27	# (index, params, output_tok_ids) tuples for new
28	# requests added to the batch.
29	AddedRequest = tuple[int, Union[SamplingParams, PoolingParams], list[int]]
30	# (index 1, index 2, directionality) tuples representing
31	# one-way moves or two-way swaps of requests in batch
32	MovedRequest = tuple[int, int, MoveDirectionality]
33	# Batch indices of any removed requests.
34	RemovedRequest = int

37	@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
38	class BatchUpdate:
39	    """Persistent batch state change info for logitsprocs"""
40	    batch_size: int  # Current num reqs in batch
41	
42	    # Metadata for requests added to, removed from, and moved
43	    # within the persistent batch.
44	    #
45	    # Note: each added request is represented as
46	    # (index, params, output_tok_ids)
47	    # Key assumption: output_tok_ids is a reference to the
48	    # request's running output tokens list; in this way
49	    # the logits processors always see the latest list of
50	    # generated tokens
51	    removed: Sequence[RemovedRequest]
52	    moved: Sequence[MovedRequest]
53	    added: Sequence[AddedRequest]
54	
55	
56	class BatchUpdateBuilder:
57	    """Helps track persistent batch state changes and build
58	    a batch update data structure for logitsprocs
59	    
60	    Assumptions:
61	    * All information about requests removed from persistent batch
62	      during a step is aggregated in self._removed through calls to
63	      self.removed_append() at the beginning of a step. This must happen
64	      before the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
65	      or self.peek_removed() are invoked in a given step
66	    * After the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
67	      or self.peek_removed() are read in a step, no new removals
68	      are registered using self.removed_append()
69	    * Elements of self._removed are never directly modified, added or
70	      removed (i.e. modification is only via self.removed_append() and
71	      self.pop_removed())
72	    
73	    Guarantees under above assumptions:
74	    * self.removed is always sorted in descending order
75	    * self.pop_removed() and self.peek_removed() both return
76	      the lowest removed request index in the current step
77	    """
78	
79	    _removed: list[RemovedRequest]
80	    _is_removed_sorted: bool
81	    moved: list[MovedRequest]
82	    added: list[AddedRequest]
83	
84	    def __init__(
85	        self,
86	        removed: Optional[list[RemovedRequest]] = None,
87	        moved: Optional[list[MovedRequest]] = None,
88	        added: Optional[list[AddedRequest]] = None,
89	    ) -> None:
90	        self._removed = removed or []
91	        self.moved = moved or []
92	        self.added = added or []
93	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
94	
95	    def _ensure_removed_sorted(self) -> None:
96	        """Sort removed request indices in
97	        descending order.
98	        
99	        Idempotent after first call in a
100	        given step, until reset.
101	        """
102	        if not self._is_removed_sorted:
103	            self._removed.sort(reverse=True)
104	            self._is_removed_sorted = True
105	
106	    @property
107	    def removed(self) -> list[RemovedRequest]:
108	        """Removed request indices sorted in
109	        descending order"""
110	        self._ensure_removed_sorted()
111	        return self._removed
112	
113	    def removed_append(self, index: int) -> None:
114	        """Register the removal of a request from
115	        the persistent batch.
116	
117	        Must not be called after the first time
118	        self.removed, self.pop_removed() or
119	        self.peek_removed() are invoked.
120	        
121	        Args:
122	          index: request index
123	        """
124	        if self._is_removed_sorted:
125	            raise RuntimeError("Cannot register new removed request after"
126	                               " self.removed has been read.")
127	        self._removed.append(index)
128	
129	    def has_removed(self) -> bool:
130	        return bool(self._removed)
131	
132	    def peek_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
133	        """Return lowest removed request index"""
134	        if self.has_removed():
135	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
136	            return self._removed[-1]
137	        return None
138	
139	    def pop_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
140	        """Pop lowest removed request index"""
141	        if self.has_removed():
142	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
143	            return self._removed.pop()
144	        return None
145	
146	    def get_and_reset(self, batch_size: int) -> Optional[BatchUpdate]:
147	        """Generate a logitsprocs batch update data structure
148	        and reset internal batch update builder state.
149	        
150	        Args:
151	          batch_size: current persistent batch size
152	
153	        Returns:
154	          Frozen logitsprocs batch update instance; `None` if no updates
155	        """
156	        # Reset removal-sorting logic
157	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
158	        if not any((self._removed, self.moved, self.added)):
159	            # No update; short-circuit
160	            return None
161	        # Build batch state update
162	        batch_update = BatchUpdate(
163	            batch_size=batch_size,
164	            removed=self._removed,
165	            moved=self.moved,
166	            added=self.added,
167	        )
168	        # Reset removed/moved/added update lists
169	        self._removed = []
170	        self.moved = []
171	        self.added = []
172	        return batch_update

197	    ) -> None:
198	        """Called when there are new output tokens, prior
199	        to each forward pass.
200	
201	        Args:
202	            batch_update is non-None iff there have been

204	        """

209	class LogitsProcessorManager:
210	    """Encapsulates initialized logitsproc objects."""

225	class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
226	
227	    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
228	                 device: DeviceLikeType):
229	        super().__init__()
230	        self.min_p_count: int = 0
231	
232	        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
233	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
234	                                            device="cpu",
235	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
236	        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
237	        # Pre-allocated device tensor
238	        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
239	                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
240	                                                      device=device)
241	        # Current slice of the device tensor
242	        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
243	
244	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
245	        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
246	        return True
247	
248	    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
249	        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])
250	
251	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
252	        if not batch_update:
253	            return
254	
255	        needs_update = False
256	        # Process added requests.
257	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
258	            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
259	            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
260	                needs_update = True
261	                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
262	            if min_p:
263	                self.min_p_count += 1
264	
265	        if self.min_p_count:
266	            # Process removed requests.
267	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
268	            for index in batch_update.removed:
269	                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
270	                    self.min_p_count -= 1
271	
272	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
273	            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
274	                change = (min_p_a :=
275	                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
276	                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
277	                needs_update |= change
278	                if change:
279	                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
280	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
281	                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b
282	
283	        # Update tensors if needed.
284	        size = batch_update.batch_size
285	        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
286	            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
287	            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
288	            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
289	
290	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
291	        if not self.min_p_count:
292	            return logits
293	
294	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
295	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
296	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
297	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
298	                                       dim=-1,
299	                                       keepdim=True)
300	        # Adjust min_p
301	        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
302	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
303	        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
304	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
305	        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
306	        return logits
307

309	class LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
310	
311	    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
312	        super().__init__()
313	        self.biases: dict[int, dict[int, float]] = {}
314	        self.device = device
315	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
316	
317	        self.bias_tensor: torch.Tensor = torch.tensor(())
318	        self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor([], torch.int32),
319	                             self._device_tensor([], torch.int32))
320	
321	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
322	        """Logit bias can rebalance token probabilities and change the
323	        outcome of argmax in greedy sampling."""
324	        return False
325	
326	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
327	        if not batch_update:
328	            return
329	
330	        # Process added requests.
331	        needs_update = bool(batch_update.added)
332	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
333	            if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) and (lb :=
334	                                                       params.logit_bias):
335	                self.biases[index] = lb
336	            else:
337	                self.biases.pop(index, None)
338	
339	        if self.biases:
340	            # Process removed requests.
341	            for index in batch_update.removed:
342	                if self.biases.pop(index, None):
343	                    needs_update = True
344	
345	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
346	            for a_index, b_index, direct in batch_update.moved:
347	                if direct == MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL:
348	                    if (a_entry := self.biases.pop(a_index, None)) is None:
349	                        if self.biases.pop(b_index, None) is not None:
350	                            needs_update = True
351	                    else:
352	                        self.biases[b_index] = a_entry
353	                        needs_update = True
354	                else:
355	                    a_entry = self.biases.pop(a_index, None)
356	                    if (b_entry := self.biases.pop(b_index, None)) is not None:
357	                        self.biases[a_index] = b_entry
358	                        needs_update = True
359	                    if a_entry is not None:
360	                        self.biases[b_index] = a_entry
361	                        needs_update = True
362	
363	        # Update tensors if needed.
364	        if needs_update:
365	            reqs, tok_ids, biases = [], [], []
366	            for req, lb in self.biases.items():
367	                reqs.extend([req] * len(lb))
368	                tok_ids.extend(lb.keys())
369	                biases.extend(lb.values())
370	
371	            self.bias_tensor = self._device_tensor(biases, torch.float32)
372	            self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor(reqs, torch.int32),
373	                                 self._device_tensor(tok_ids, torch.int32))
374	
375	    def _device_tensor(self, data: list, dtype: torch.dtype) -> torch.Tensor:
376	        return (torch.tensor(data,
377	                             device="cpu",
378	                             dtype=dtype,
379	                             pin_memory=self.pin_memory).to(device=self.device,
380	                                                            non_blocking=True))
381	
382	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
383	        if self.biases:
384	            logits[self.logits_slice] += self.bias_tensor
385	        return logits

388	class MinTokensLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
389	
390	    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
391	        # index -> (min_toks, output_token_ids, stop_token_ids)
392	        super().__init__()
393	        self.min_toks: dict[int, tuple[int, Sequence[int], set[int]]] = {}
394	        self.device = device
395	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
396	
397	        # (req_idx_tensor,eos_tok_id_tensor)
398	        self.logits_slice: tuple[torch.Tensor,
399	                                 torch.Tensor] = (self._device_tensor(
400	                                     [], torch.int32),
401	                                                  self._device_tensor(
402	                                                      [], torch.int32))
403	
404	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
405	        """By censoring stop tokens, min-tokens can change the outcome
406	        of the argmax operation in greedy sampling."""
407	        return False
408	
409	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
410	        needs_update = False
411	
412	        if batch_update:
413	            # Process added requests.
414	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.added)
415	            for index, params, output_tok_ids in batch_update.added:
416	                if (isinstance(params, SamplingParams)
417	                        and (min_tokens := params.min_tokens)
418	                        and len(output_tok_ids) < min_tokens):
419	                    # Replace request metadata at batch index
420	                    self.min_toks[index] = (min_tokens, output_tok_ids,
421	                                            params.all_stop_token_ids)
422	                else:
423	                    # Drop request metadata at batch index
424	                    self.min_toks.pop(index, None)
425	
426	            if self.min_toks:
427	                # Process removed requests.
428	                for index in batch_update.removed:
429	                    if self.min_toks.pop(index, None):
430	                        needs_update = True
431	
432	                # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and
433	                # swapped (a<->b)
434	                for a_index, b_index, direct in batch_update.moved:
435	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL:
436	                        if (a_entry := self.min_toks.pop(a_index,
437	                                                         None)) is None:
438	                            if self.min_toks.pop(b_index, None) is not None:
439	                                needs_update = True
440	                        else:
441	                            self.min_toks[b_index] = a_entry
442	                            needs_update = True
443	                    else:
444	                        a_entry = self.min_toks.pop(a_index, None)
445	                        if (b_entry := self.min_toks.pop(b_index,
446	                                                         None)) is not None:
447	                            self.min_toks[a_index] = b_entry
448	                            needs_update = True
449	                        if a_entry is not None:
450	                            self.min_toks[b_index] = a_entry
451	                            needs_update = True
452	
453	        if self.min_toks:
454	            # Check for any requests that have attained their min tokens.
455	            to_remove = tuple(index for index, (min_toks, out_tok_ids,
456	                                                _) in self.min_toks.items()
457	                              if len(out_tok_ids) >= min_toks)
458	            if to_remove:
459	                needs_update = True
460	                for index in to_remove:
461	                    del self.min_toks[index]
462	
463	        # Update tensors if needed.
464	        if needs_update:
465	            reqs: list[int] = []
466	            tok_ids: list[int] = []
467	            for req, (_, _, stop_tok_ids) in self.min_toks.items():
468	                reqs.extend([req] * len(stop_tok_ids))
469	                tok_ids.extend(stop_tok_ids)
470	
471	            self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor(reqs, torch.int32),
472	                                 self._device_tensor(tok_ids, torch.int32))
473	
474	    def _device_tensor(self, data: list, dtype: torch.dtype) -> torch.Tensor:
475	        return (torch.tensor(data,
476	                             device="cpu",
477	                             dtype=dtype,
478	                             pin_memory=self.pin_memory).to(device=self.device,
479	                                                            non_blocking=True))
480	
481	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
482	        if self.min_toks:
483	            # Inhibit EOS token for requests which have not reached min length
484	            logits[self.logits_slice] = -float("inf")
485	        return logits

488	def init_builtin_logitsprocs(pin_memory_available: bool, max_num_reqs: int,
489	                             device: torch.device) -> LogitsProcessorManager:
490	    """Construct 'builtin' vLLM logitsprocs which the engine
491	    loads by default.
492	
493	    Args:
494	      pin_memory_available: pinned memory is available for use
495	                            for use by logitsproc
496	      max_num_reqs: ceiling on request count in persistent batch
497	      device: inference device
498	
499	    Returns:
500	      Data structure encapsulating loaded logitsprocs
501	    """
502	    min_tokens_logitproc = MinTokensLogitsProcessor(
503	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
504	    logit_bias_logitproc = LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(
505	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
506	    min_p_logitproc = MinPLogitsProcessor(
507	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available,
508	        device=device,
509	        # +1 for temporary swap space
510	        max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1)
511	    return LogitsProcessorManager(
512	        non_argmax_invariant=[
513	            min_tokens_logitproc,
514	            logit_bias_logitproc,
515	        ],
516	        argmax_invariant=[min_p_logitproc],
517	    )

33	class OpenAIBaseModel(BaseModel):
34	    # OpenAI API does allow extra fields
35	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
36	
37	    # Cache class field names
38	    field_names: ClassVar[Optional[set[str]]] = None
39	
40	    @model_validator(mode="wrap")
41	    @classmethod
42	    def __log_extra_fields__(cls, data, handler):
43	        result = handler(data)
44	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
45	            return result
46	        field_names = cls.field_names
47	        if field_names is None:
48	            # Get all class field names and their potential aliases
49	            field_names = set()
50	            for field_name, field in cls.model_fields.items():
51	                field_names.add(field_name)
52	                if alias := getattr(field, "alias", None):
53	                    field_names.add(alias)
54	            cls.field_names = field_names
55	
56	        # Compare against both field names and aliases
57	        if any(k not in field_names for k in data):
58	            logger.warning(
59	                "The following fields were present in the request "
60	                "but ignored: %s",
61	                data.keys() - field_names,
62	            )
63	        return result

951	        return SamplingParams.from_optional(

983	    @model_validator(mode="before")

985	    def check_guided_decoding_count(cls, data):
986	        guide_count = sum([
987	            "guided_json" in data and data["guided_json"] is not None,
988	            "guided_regex" in data and data["guided_regex"] is not None,

12	logger = init_logger(__name__)

15	    import flashinfer.sampling
16	    is_flashinfer_available = True
17	except ImportError:
18	    is_flashinfer_available = False

21	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
22	    """
23	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
24	    weighted random sampling of logits.
25	
26	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
27	    """
28	
29	    def __init__(self):
30	        super().__init__()
31	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
32	            if is_flashinfer_available:
33	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
34	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
35	                    logger.warning(
36	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
37	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
38	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
39	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
40	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
41	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
42	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
43	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
44	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
45	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
46	                    # why we use the condition
47	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
48	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
49	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
50	                else:
51	                    logger.warning(
52	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
53	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
54	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
55	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
56	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
57	            else:
58	                logger.warning(
59	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
60	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
61	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
62	                self.forward = self.forward_native
63	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
64	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
65	        else:
66	            self.forward = self.forward_native
67	
68	    def forward_native(
69	        self,
70	        logits: torch.Tensor,
71	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
72	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
73	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
75	        """
76	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
77	
78	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
79	        """
80	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
81	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
82	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
83	
84	    def forward_cuda(
85	        self,
86	        logits: torch.Tensor,
87	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
88	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
89	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
91	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
92	        if k is None and p is None:
93	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
94	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
95	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
96	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
97	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
98	        if generators:
99	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
100	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
101	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
102	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
103	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
104	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
105	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
106	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
107	
108	    def forward_tpu(
109	        self,
110	        logits: torch.Tensor,
111	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
112	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
113	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
115	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
116	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
117	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
118	
119	
120	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
121	    logits: torch.Tensor,
122	    k: torch.Tensor,
123	    p: torch.Tensor,
124	) -> torch.Tensor:
125	    """
126	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
127	
128	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
129	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
130	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
131	    shall constitute the top-p set.
132	
133	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
134	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
135	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
136	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
137	    being broken then.
138	    """
139	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
140	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
141	
142	    if k is not None:
143	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
144	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
145	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
146	
147	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
148	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
149	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

40	def _prepare_test(
41	        batch_size: int
42	) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, MockLogitsSampler]:
43	    input_tensor = torch.rand((batch_size, 1024), dtype=torch.float16)
44	    fake_logits = torch.full((batch_size, VOCAB_SIZE),
45	                             1e-2,

54	    f"cuda:{i}" for i in range(1 if torch.cuda.device_count() == 1 else 2)

58	def _do_sample(
59	    batch_size: int,
60	    input_tensor: torch.Tensor,
61	    sampler: MockLogitsSampler,
62	    sampling_params: SamplingParams,
63	    device: str,
64	):
65	    seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata] = []
66	    seq_lens: list[int] = []
67	    for i in range(batch_size):
68	        seq_group_metadata_list.append(
69	            SequenceGroupMetadata(
70	                request_id=f"test_{i}",
71	                is_prompt=True,
72	                seq_data={0: SequenceData.from_seqs([1, 2, 3])},
73	                sampling_params=sampling_params,
74	                block_tables={0: [1]},
75	            ))
76	        seq_lens.append(seq_group_metadata_list[-1].seq_data[0].get_len())
77	
78	    sampling_metadata = SamplingMetadata.prepare(
79	        seq_group_metadata_list,
80	        seq_lens,
81	        query_lens=seq_lens,
82	        device=device,
83	        pin_memory=is_pin_memory_available())
84	    return sampler(logits=input_tensor, sampling_metadata=sampling_metadata)

27	def _create_fake_logits(batch_size: int, vocab_size: int) -> torch.Tensor:
28	    fake_logits = torch.full((batch_size, vocab_size), 1e-2, dtype=torch.float)
29	    return fake_logits

32	def _create_penalty_tensor(batch_size: int, penalty_value: float,
33	                           device: torch.device) -> torch.Tensor:
34	    return torch.full((batch_size, ),
35	                      fill_value=penalty_value,
36	                      dtype=torch.float,
37	                      device=device)
38	
39	
40	def _create_prompt_tokens_tensor(
41	    prompt_token_ids: list[list[int]],
42	    vocab_size: int,
43	    device: torch.device,
44	) -> torch.Tensor:
45	    return make_tensor_with_pad(
46	        prompt_token_ids,
47	        pad=vocab_size,
48	        device=device,
49	        dtype=torch.int64,
50	        pin_memory=False,
51	    )
52	
53	
54	def _create_allowed_token_ids(
55	    batch_size: int,
56	    vocab_size: int,
57	    num_allowed_token_ids: int,
58	    device: torch.device,
59	) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
60	    mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
61	    for i in range(batch_size):
62	        if i % 2 == 1:
63	            continue
64	        if mask is None:
65	            mask = torch.zeros((batch_size, vocab_size),
66	                               dtype=torch.bool,
67	                               device=device)
68	        start = min(i, vocab_size - 1)
69	        end = min(i + num_allowed_token_ids, vocab_size - 1)
70	        mask[i, start:end] = True
71	    return mask
72	
73	
74	def _create_bad_words_token_ids(
75	        batch_size: int, vocab_size: int,
76	        bad_words_lengths: list[tuple[int]]) -> dict[int, list[list[int]]]:
77	    bad_words_token_ids = {}
78	    for batch_idx in range(batch_size):
79	        token_ids_single_batch = []
80	        for bad_words_length in bad_words_lengths:
81	            token_ids = np.random.choice(vocab_size,
82	                                         size=bad_words_length,
83	                                         replace=True).tolist()
84	            token_ids_single_batch.append(token_ids)
85	        bad_words_token_ids[batch_idx] = token_ids_single_batch
86	    if batch_size >= 2:
87	        # Test no bad_words for some batch
88	        no_bad_words_batch_idx = np.random.choice(batch_size)
89	        bad_words_token_ids.pop(no_bad_words_batch_idx, None)
90	    return bad_words_token_ids
91	
92	
93	def _update_output_token_ids_for_bad_words(
94	        metadata: SamplingMetadata, vocab_size: int) -> dict[int, list[int]]:
95	    bad_words_last_tokens = {}
96	    for batch_idx, bad_words_token_ids in metadata.bad_words_token_ids.items():
97	        output_token_ids = metadata.output_token_ids[batch_idx]
98	        bad_words_last_token: list[int] = []
99	        for i, bad_word_token_ids in enumerate(bad_words_token_ids):
100	            if len(bad_word_token_ids) == 1:
101	                # Single token id always affects logits
102	                bad_words_last_token.append(bad_word_token_ids[0])
103	            else:
104	                prefix_length = len(bad_word_token_ids) - 1
105	                has_bad_words = np.random.choice([True, False])
106	                if has_bad_words:
107	                    output_token_ids[-prefix_length:] = bad_word_token_ids[:-1]
108	                    bad_words_last_token.append(bad_word_token_ids[-1])
109	                    break  # Maximum one update to output_token_ids
110	                else:  # Make sure no accidental match to bad words
111	                    output_token_ids[-1] = (bad_word_token_ids[-2] +
112	                                            1) % vocab_size
113	        bad_words_last_tokens[batch_idx] = bad_words_last_token
114	    return bad_words_last_tokens

159
